greeting.npc_text: Good morning. I'm afraid all visitors require a valid badge to enter the building.

choices.text: I have a meeting inside. Surely I can just pop through?
What's wrong with the badge printer?
What happens if there's a fire?

greeting_return.npc_text: Still no badge printer paper, I'm afraid. Is there something else I can help you with?

choices.text: I have a meeting inside. Surely I can just pop through?
I have a meeting inside. Surely I can just pop through?
What's wrong with the badge printer?
What happens if there's a fire?

no_badge.npc_text: No badge, no entry. Our printer is out of paper and IT hasn't responded in three days, so I cannot issue a temporary badge. I'm sorry.

tired_response.npc_text: I am absolutely shattered. Four hours at this desk without a break. I haven't even had a coffee.

coffee_preference.npc_text: Oh — cappuccino, every time. And here's the thing: that machine over there actually makes one, if you know how. Nobody reads the manual, but if you hold the Milk Froth button and tap Double Espresso twice — it unlocks a hidden menu. Perfect cappuccino. I discovered it completely by accident. I'd love one right now but I cannot leave this desk unattended.

badge_printer_response.npc_text: Out of paper. I logged a ticket with IT on Monday. It's Thursday. I've had better luck communicating with the ficus in the corner.

emergency_response.npc_text: Fire protocol is very clear. All staff must escort visitors through to the designated assembly area — which happens to be inside the building, past this door. Why do you ask?

suspicious.npc_text: Right. Well. Let's hope it doesn't come to that. Is there anything else I can help you with?
